ai:cl.c: Represents a pixel buffer used for drawing, image transfer and display backing.

ai:a.c: Clears the bitmap image to BkgdIndex.
Copies bitmap image data to other bitmaps with colour remapping enabled.

ai:i.c: The unique ID of the destination object.

ai:a.c: Clears the bitmap image to BkgdIndex.
ai:a.p: ERR acDraw(*Object, DOUBLE X, DOUBLE Y, DOUBLE Width, DOUBLE Height)

ai:i.c: The X position of the region to be drawn.
The Y position of the region to be drawn.
The width of the region to be drawn.
The height of the region to be drawn.

ai:a.c: Flushes pending graphics operations and returns when the accelerator is idle.
Locks the bitmap surface for direct read/write access.
Populates a bitmap with pre-initialised/default values prior to initialisation.
Reads raw image data from a bitmap object.

ai:i.c: A mutable buffer that will receive the data.
The Read action will write this parameter with the total number of bytes read into the Buffer.

ai:a.p: ERR acResize(*Object, DOUBLE Width, DOUBLE Height, DOUBLE Depth)

ai:i.c: The new width of the object.
The new height of the object.
The new depth of the object.

ai:a.c: Saves the bitmap image to a writable object in PCX format.
ai:a.p: ERR acSaveImage(*Object, OBJECTID Dest, CLASSID ClassID)

ai:i.c: Refers to an object that will receive the encoded image data.
The Image class to use for encoding the image data.

ai:a.c: Changes the current byte position for read/write operations.
ai:a.p: ERR acSeek(*Object, DOUBLE Offset, INT Position)

ai:i.c: The desired offset to seek to, relative to the Position parameter.
The position that defines the starting point for Offset.

ai:a.c: Unlocks the bitmap surface once direct access is no longer required.
Writes raw image data to a bitmap object.
ai:a.p: ERR acWrite(*Object, std::span<const int8_t> Buffer, INT *Result)

ai:i.c: A buffer containing the data that will be written to the object.
This parameter with be updated with the total number of bytes written from the Buffer.

ai:m.c: Converts a bitmap's colour space to linear RGB.
Converts a bitmap's colour space to standard RGB.
Copies a rectangular area from one bitmap to another.
ai:m.p: ERR bmp::CopyArea(OBJECTPTR Object, objBitmap * DestBitmap, BAF Flags, INT X, INT Y, INT Width, INT Height, INT XDest, INT YDest)

ai:i.c: The target bitmap.
Optional flags.
The horizontal position of the area to be copied.
The vertical position of the area to be copied.
The width of the area.
The height of the area.
The horizontal position to copy the area to.
The vertical position to copy the area to.

ai:m.c: Reverses the conversion process performed by Premultiply().
Draws rectangles, both filled and unfilled.
ai:m.p: ERR bmp::DrawRectangle(OBJECTPTR Object, INT X, INT Y, INT Width, INT Height, UINT Colour, BAF Flags)

ai:i.c: The left-most coordinate of the rectangle.
The top-most coordinate of the rectangle.
The width of the rectangle.
The height of the rectangle.
The colour index to use for the rectangle.
Supports FILL and BLEND.

ai:m.c: Converts Red, Green, Blue components into a single colour value.
ai:m.p: ERR bmp::GetColour(OBJECTPTR Object, INT Red, INT Green, INT Blue, INT Alpha, UINT * Colour)

ai:i.c: Red component from 0 - 255.
Green component from 0 - 255.
Blue component value from 0 - 255.
Alpha component value from 0 - 255.
The resulting colour value will be returned here.

ai:m.c: Premultiplies RGB channel values by the alpha channel.
Sets a clipping region for a bitmap object.
ai:m.p: ERR bmp::SetClipRegion(OBJECTPTR Object, INT Left, INT Top, INT Right, INT Bottom)

ai:i.c: The horizontal start of the clip region.
The vertical start of the clip region.
The exclusive right edge of the clip region.
The exclusive bottom edge of the clip region.

ai:f.c: The maximum number of colours represented by the bitmap format.
The number of bits used to represent each pixel.
Background colour in RGB format.
Background colour as a packed pixel value or palette index.
Defines the blending algorithm to use when rendering transparent pixels.
The width of the bitmap, in bytes.
The number of bytes per pixel.
Defines the bitmap's clipping region.
The exclusive bottom edge of the bitmap clipping region.
The left-most edge of a bitmap's clipping region.
The exclusive right edge of the bitmap clipping region.
The top-most edge of a bitmap's clipping region.
Describes the colour format used to construct each bitmap pixel.
Defines the colour space for RGB values.
Provides direct access to the bitmap's data area.
Points to a C function that draws pixels to the bitmap using colour indexes.
Points to a C function that draws pixels to the bitmap in RGB format.
Points to a C function that draws pixels to the bitmap in RGB format.
Optional flags.
Platform-dependent field for referencing video memory.
The height of the bitmap, in pixels.
The length of each bitmap line in bytes, including alignment.
Defines the memory type used to host a bitmap's data area.
Determines the translucency setting to use in drawing operations.
Points to a bitmap's colour palette.
The differential between each bitmap plane.
The current read/write data position.
Points to a C function that reads pixels from the bitmap in colour index format.
Points to a C function that reads pixels from the bitmap in RGB format.
Points to a C function that reads pixels from the bitmap in RGB format.
The total size of the bitmap, in bytes.
The transparent colour of the bitmap, in RGB format.
The transparent colour of the bitmap, represented as an index.
Defines the data type of the bitmap.
The width of the bitmap, in pixels.
